video:video.video:title: Vermont flower farmer reacts to recent temperature swings
video:video.video:description: An abrupt swing from hot weather to cold across the Northeast is frustrating some flower and fruit farmers who have had to either harvest blooms extra early or fear they could lose some crops altogether.

video:video.video:title: Anne Hathaway and Meryl Streep premiere 'The Devil Wears Prada 2'
video:video.video:description: The long-awaited sequel to 2006 hit "The Devil Wears Prada" is given a glitzy world premiere in New York. (April 21)

video:video.video:title: Meryl Streep's Miranda Priestly isn't based on who you think
video:video.video:description: Forget Anna Wintour - Meryl Streep explains who she was really channeling when portraying Miranda Priestly in "The Devil Wears Prada."

video:video.video:title: 'The Devil Wears Prada' exceeded Anne Hathaway's expectations
video:video.video:description: Who's the most surprised "The Devil Wears Prada" got a sequel? Anne Hathaway still can't believe it.

video:video.video:title: How Jessica Sula met La Toya Jackson
video:video.video:description: A meet-cute for "Michael" actor Jessica Sula and La Toya Jackson - who she plays in the biopic - at the LA premiere.
video:video.video:tag: Jessica Sula La Toya Jackson Toya

video:video.video:title: EU transport ministers discuss possibility of fuel shortages and readiness
video:video.video:description: Speaking ahead of a meeting with transportation ministers from across the European Union, Cyprus Transport Minister Alexis Vafeades, who is chairing Tuesday's meeting, stressed that fuel shortages are not yet happening in the EU, but that it could happen because of the situation in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Japan approves scrapping a ban on lethal weapons exports in a change of its postwar pacifist policy
video:video.video:description: Japan on Tuesday endorsed scrapping a ban on lethal weapons exports, a major change of its postwar pacifist policy as the country seeks to build up its arms industry and deepen cooperation with defense partners.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 21
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, April 21st: U.S.-Iran ceasefire expires Wednesday; Officials says Israel and Lebanon to meet Thursday; Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer leaves Trump’s cabinet; Virginia to vote on Congressional redistricting.

video:video.video:title: Producer Graham King talks 'Michael' reshoots
video:video.video:description: "Michael" producer Graham King says reshoots on the biopic were a positive thing, as the creative team decided they needed more levity and fun at the core of the film.

video:video.video:title: The Jacksons get emotional at 'Michael' premiere
video:video.video:description: As Jaafar Jackson takes on the role of his famous uncle in biopic "Michael," the rest of the family including La Toya, Marlon and Jackie talk about seeing the film and misconceptions about their childhood.

video:video.video:title: Finland's duo feeling the spirit of Eurovision
video:video.video:description: Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen, Finland's 2026 Eurovision hopefuls, describe the feeling of camaraderie at the contest.

video:video.video:title: Trump labor secretary leaving Cabinet after abuse of power allegations
video:video.video:description: abor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer is out of President Donald Trump’s Cabinet, the White House said Monday, after multiple allegations of abusing her position’s power, including having an affair with a subordinate and drinking alcohol on the job.

video:video.video:title: US forces board sanctioned oil tanker in Indian Ocean
video:video.video:description: U.S. forces have boarded an oil tanker previously sanctioned for smuggling Iranian crude oil in Asia, the Department of Defense said.

video:video.video:title: The Onion launches new bid to take over Alex Jones' Infowars and turn it into a parody platform
video:video.video:description: Satirical news outlet The Onion has a new plan to take over conspiracy theorist Alex Jones' Infowars platforms and turn them into parodies. (Produced by Elaine Carroll)

video:video.video:title: As the planet warms, scientists burn homes to figure out how to best protect them in wildfires
video:video.video:description: Scientists at the Insurance Institute for Business & Home Safety burn homes to figure out how to best protect them in wildfires. (AP video: Erik Verduzco)

video:video.video:title: NATO intercepts Russian military aircraft flying over the Baltic Sea
video:video.video:description: NATO intercepted Russian strategic bombers and fighter jets that flew over the Baltic Sea on Monday, a muscular display of air power on the alliance’s eastern flank away from the spotlight on the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Austrian Eurovision Song Contest stars meet the country's president ahead of 2026 event in Vienna
video:video.video:description: Austria's past Eurovision winners Conchita Wurst and JJ meet this year's representative, Cosmó, at a reception event in Vienna with the Austrian president Alexander Van der Bellen.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo speaks about the legacy of Pope Francis on the flight to Equatorial Guinea
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV paid tribute to Pope Francis on Tuesday on the first anniversary of his death, recalling his preaching about God’s mercy and his gestures of solidarity with poor people.

video:video.video:title: Video shows armed man shooting at tourists at Teotihuacán pyramids near Mexico City
video:video.video:description: An armed man standing atop one of the historic Teotihuacán pyramids opened fire on tourists Monday, killing one Canadian and leaving at least 13 people injured at the archaeological site north of Mexico’s capital, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Scenes in Tehran as US and Iran signal they will return to Pakistan for ceasefire talks
video:video.video:description: With the 14-day ceasefire set to expire Wednesday, uncertainty is growing over possible negotiations between Iran and United States.

video:video.video:title: Come on an AP tour of the newly found location of Shakespeare’s London house
video:video.video:description: A newly discovered 17th-century map sheds new light on Shakespeare’s London life, pinpointing for the first time the exact location of the only home he bought in the city, and where he may have worked on his final plays.

video:video.video:title: Virginia voters deciding on redistricting plan that could boost Democrats' seats in Congress
video:video.video:description: A proposed constitutional amendment would bypass the state's bipartisan redistricting commission to allow use of new congressional districts approved by state lawmakers in this year's midterm elections.

video:video.video:title: Faithful flock to visit Pope Francis' tomb one year after his death
video:video.video:description: One year after the death of Pope Francis, thousands of faithful still line up every day to pass by his tomb.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV speaks about the legacy of his predecessor Francis on the flight to Equatorial Guinea
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV paid tribute to Pope Francis on Tuesday on the first anniversary of his death, recalling his preaching about God’s mercy and his gestures of solidarity with poor people.

video:video.video:title: Devastation in Ukrainian city under attack by Russian forces
video:video.video:description: Drone footage filmed by the Ukrainian Armed Forces showed devastation in the eastern Ukrainian city of Chasiv Yar.

video:video.video:title: Trump’s Cuba threats revive exile hopes, fears over property claims
video:video.video:description: Among Cuban American exiles and their descendants in Miami, hopes have stirred that 2026 could finally be the year of regime change on the communist-run island, and some hope that their property confiscated after the Cuban revolution could be returned if a new government comes to power.

video:video.video:title: Priceless 2,500-year-old golden helmet returned to Romania after Dutch museum raid
video:video.video:description: A priceless golden helmet dating back 2,500 years was returned to Romania on Tuesday after the national heirloom was stolen from a Dutch museum where it was on loan last year.

video:video.video:title: The AP Interview: Cyprus president says EU needs a clear playbook on helping members under attack
video:video.video:description: European Union leaders meeting in Cyprus need to start preparing a playbook on what should happen if an EU country under attack puts out a call for help from bloc partners, the president of Cyprus said.

video:video.video:title: Timelapse video shows movement of ships in and around the Strait of Hormuz over 24 hours
video:video.video:description: When the United States and Israel attacked Iran on February 28, Iran retaliated with attacks that effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Witness says he jumped off the side of the pyramid during shooting in Mexico
video:video.video:description: A tourist who was caught up in a shooting at Mexico’s iconic Teotihuacan pyramids described the "chaos" as a gunman opened fire at a crowd of visitors at the archaeological site on Monday.

video:video.video:title: 2 ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz, complicating diplomatic efforts to resume talks
video:video.video:description: 2 ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz, complicating diplomatic efforts to resume talks

video:video.video:title: Prince's first wife Mayte on keeping his legacy alive a decade after his death | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Ten years after Prince's death, his first wife Mayte Jannell Garcia has relaunched the Live 4 Love charity that she and the pop star first established after the 1996 death of their infant child.

video:video.video:title: Researchers create new potato varieties to strengthen nation’s chip supply
video:video.video:description: Researchers spend decades developing potatoes for chip makers that can grow in all kinds of climates, avoid diseases and pests, sit in storage for months and still deliver a satisfying crunch (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Iranians in Turkey trapped in limbo as war raises the stakes of an uncertain existence
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of Iranians live in neighboring Turkey, drawn by economic opportunities and relative stability.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 22
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, April 22nd: Iran fires at container ship in Strait of Hormuz; Virginia passes House redistricting plan; Southern Poverty Law Center accused of funneling money to hate group leaders; Hegseth says military personnel won’t have to get flu vaccine.

video:video.video:title: How luxury watchmaking has become a fabric of Switzerland's heritage
video:video.video:description: Switzerland’s reputation for watchmaking has been built over centuries, with Geneva at the center of a global luxury industry defined by precision engineering and specialist craftsmanship.

video:video.video:title: Florida's attorney general launches criminal probe into ChatGPT over FSU shooting
video:video.video:description: Florida's attorney general has opened an investigation into OpenAI's ChatGPT. The probe examines the AI app's interactions with a gunman charged with a fatal shooting at Florida State University last year.

video:video.video:title: Trump's approval on economy falls during Iran war new AP-NORC poll shows
video:video.video:description: Trump’s approval rating on the economy dropped to 30% in April from 38% in a March AP-NORC poll. (AP Video by Nathan Ellgren)

video:video.video:title: No cuddles, but lots of care: How a Paris-area wildlife hospital keeps rescued animals wild
video:video.video:description: In a southeastern suburb of Paris, a wildlife hospital cares for injured, sick and orphaned animals — often victims of human activity and increasing urbanization.

video:video.video:title: Trump and top Republicans to read Bible passages at “America Reads the Bible” event
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump and top Republicans are taking part in a marathon Bible reading this week as part of an America 250-themed event promoting a “return to the spiritual foundation” of the U.S.

video:video.video:title: AP Explains: Why the Bible passage Trump will read aloud is so potent and polarizing
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump plans to read a notable Bible passage from the Oval Office in a livestreamed marathon.

video:video.video:title: Emotional Scenes outside criminal sentencing for OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma
video:video.video:description: A U.S. District Judge on Tuesday delayed the criminal sentencing of OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma in order to allow victims to attend the court proceeding in person.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories for April 21
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, April 21st: President Trump extends ceasefire with Iran; Teotihuacan pyramids gunman had materials related to Columbine; A Taiwanese company is building drones; Three members of Japan's GSDT died in explosion

video:video.video:title: Gunman who fired on tourists at pyramids in Mexico was influence by violent acts in US
video:video.video:description: The gunman who opened fire on tourists at Mexico’s iconic Teotihuacan pyramids carried materials that were apparently related to the deadly 1999 shooting at Columbine High School, authorities said Tuesday, a day after the attack that killed a Canadian woman and left at least 13 people injured.

video:video.video:title: Funerals held for Hezbollah combatants in southern Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Thousands of mourners gathered in southern Lebanon on Tuesday for the funerals of Hezbollah fighters killed in the latest round of violence between the militant group and Israel, as communities across the region continue to bury their dead.

video:video.video:title: New York police rescue bear stranded in a tree
video:video.video:description: A black bear was rescued from a tree in Albany, New York, Tuesday, after law enforcement arrived. The bear tumbled from the tree into a net after it was tranquilized.

video:video.video:title: Southern Poverty Law Center charged with defrauding donors with payments to extremist informants
video:video.video:description: The Southern Poverty Law Center was indicted Tuesday on federal fraud charges alleging it improperly raised millions of dollars to pay informants to infiltrate the Ku Klux Klan and other extremist groups, acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said.

video:video.video:title: Robots best half-marathon world record in China
video:video.video:description: A humanoid robot that won a half-marathon race in Beijing ran faster than the human world record. The winner from Honor, a Chinese smartphone maker, completed the 13-mile race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds

video:video.video:title: Jeanie Buss on the rise of women in sports
video:video.video:description: Jeanie Buss talks the rise of women in sports at the "Running Point" premiere. The Netflix series is loosely based on her life as CEO of the Los Angeles Lakers.

video:video.video:title: Judge delays OxyContin-maker Purdue Pharma's sentencing to let opioid victims attend in person
video:video.video:description: A judge on Tuesday delayed the criminal sentencing of OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma in order to allow victims to attend the court proceeding in person.

video:video.video:title: D4vd pleads not guilty to murder charge in death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez
video:video.video:description: Singer D4vd has pleaded not guilty to a murder charge in the death of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez, who was last known to be alive nearly a year ago and whose dismembered and decomposed body was found in the entertainer’s apparently abandoned Tesla.

video:video.video:title: A look back at the 2006 premiere of 'The Devil Wears Prada'
video:video.video:description: As the stars of "The Devil Wears Prada" prepare for the release of the highly-awaited sequel, we look back at the premiere of the first film 20 years ago.

video:video.video:title: Pennsylvania History Teacher Named 2026 National Teacher of the Year
video:video.video:description: Leon Smith, a teacher at Haverford High School, hopes his work inspires the next generation to become educators and make a lasting impact in their communities.

video:video.video:title: Trump renews his call for Congress to pass legislation to control college sports
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump repeated his call for Congress to pass legislation that would rein in college sports at a time athletes are allowed to move freely from school to school and command salaries that put athletic departments in financial peril.

video:video.video:title: Trump's approval on U.S. economy falls during Iran war, AP-NORC poll shows
video:video.video:description: A new AP-NORC poll finds that President Donald Trump’s approval rating on the economy has slumped over the past month as the Iran war drives prices higher.

video:video.video:title: Mother of killed Gaza hostage on loss of her son and how she navigates grief
video:video.video:description: When Hersh Goldberg-Polin was in the tunnels in Gaza after being kidnapped from a music festival, released hostages recalled he often quoted a line from Holocaust survivor Viktor Frankl: “Those who have a ‘why’ to live, can bear with almost any ‘how.’”

video:video.video:title: Jaafar Jackson on mastering the moonwalk for 'Michael'
video:video.video:description: Jaafar Jackson details how he mastered each moonwalk from various eras of Michael Jackson's career. He plays his superstar uncle in the biopic “Michael.”

video:video.video:title: Laverne Cox warns of 'dehumanization' of transgender people
video:video.video:description: Laverne Cox sees her new animated adaptation of George Orwell's "Animal Farm" as a warning against "dehumanization" — including of transgender people. (April 21)

video:video.video:title: War in Sudan leaves widespread devastation and nearly 60-thousand deaths as it enters its 4th year
video:video.video:description: Three years into the war and a year after Khartoum was taken over by the Sudanese army, the battered heart of the capital city remains quiet.

video:video.video:title: South Korean police seek to arrest K-pop mogul behind BTS
video:video.video:description: South Korean police said Tuesday they are seeking to arrest music mogul Bang Si-Hyuk, chairman of the agency behind K-pop supergroup BTS, as they expand an investigation into allegations that he illegally gained more than $100 million in an investor fraud scheme.

video:video.video:title: Celeste Rivas Hernandez's family arrives in court after D4vd's murder charge
video:video.video:description: The family of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, the 14-year-old girl whose body was found in D4vd's Tesla, arrives at a Los Angeles court following the singer's murder charge.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV welcomed by President Obiang as he arrives in Equatorial Guinea
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV arrived Tuesday in Equatorial Guinea on the fourth and final leg of his Africa journey.

video:video.video:title: Prince's first wife keeps his legacy alive with music charity
video:video.video:description: On the 10th anniversary of his death, Prince's first wife Mayte Jannell Garcia explains why she is relaunching their Live 4 Love Charities.

video:video.video:title: Iran displays what looks like ballistic missile launchers during pro-military rallies in Tehran
video:video.video:description: Iranian hard-liners rallied late Tuesday night as possible talks in Islamabad with the United States broke down, with members of Iran's paramilitary Revolutionary Guard apparently bringing ballistic missiles to events in Tehran.

video:video.video:title: Senators on Federal Reserve nominee Kevin Warsh's chances of being confirmed
video:video.video:description: Republican and Democratic U.S. Senators weigh in on Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s nominee to chair the Federal Reserve and replace current Fed chair Jerome Powell.

video:video.video:title: Long lines at cannabis stores to celebrate 4/20
video:video.video:description: April 20 marks marijuana culture’s high holiday, the unofficial holiday for cannabis.

video:video.video:title: Carpenters in Gaza are recycling used wood found under rubble to rebuild
video:video.video:description: As Israeli restrictions on entry of goods and commodities into Gaza Strip continue, carpenters have resorted to recycling used wood and pallets to serve a community, stricken by two years of war between Hamas and Israel.

video:video.video:title: 'This is a wild animal that wants to die' - Marine biologist
video:video.video:description: A marine biologist said Monday a stranded whale off the Baltic Sea coast in Germany "has no chance of survival."

video:video.video:title: As US-Iran ceasefire deadline nears, uncertainty hangs over possible talks
video:video.video:description: Last-minute ceasefire talks between the United States and Iran look uncertain as a two-week truce is set to expire and both sides say they’re prepared to resume fighting

video:video.video:title: Emotional scene outside criminal sentencing for OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma
video:video.video:description: A judge is expected to sentence OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma to forfeit $225 million to the U.S. government, a major step toward the company finalizing a settlement of nearly all of the thousands of lawsuits it faces over its role in the opioid crisis.

video:video.video:title: Semafor World Economy takes place in Washington
video:video.video:description: From artificial intelligence to supply chain, the Semafor World Economy summit invites business leaders and policy makes to talk about current events.

video:video.video:title: Mentored by Tim Cook, Apple's new CEO Ternus is a low profile hardware veteran
video:video.video:description: Ternus will take over as chief executive in September for Tim Cook, who turned Apple into a $4 trillion tech colossus during his 15-year run after the death of co-founder Steve Jobs.

video:video.video:title: Food ‘Would you rather’ with Chef José Andrés
video:video.video:description: Chef José Andrés shares with The AP his picks for ‘would you rather’ food edition at the Semafor World Economy summit.

video:video.video:title: Earth Day: What AP climate reporters do to reduce their impact
video:video.video:description: Climate reporters spend all day covering how the planet is changing. So for Earth Day, we asked them what they do in their daily lives to reduce their personal impact on the environment.

video:video.video:title: Senate Banking Committee quizzes Fed nominee Warsh over economy, independence
video:video.video:description: Senators on Tuesday quizzed President Donald Trump’s nominee to chair the Federal Reserve about whether he is able to stay independent of the president.

video:video.video:title: New Orleans recycling group turns glass bottles into sand in fight against land loss
video:video.video:description: A New Orleans company has created a system that turns glass bottles into sand to help fight Louisiana's land loss crisis. (AP Video: Stephen Smith)

video:video.video:title: Iran attacks ship in Strait of Hormuz after Trump indefinitely extends the ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Iran fired on a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, damaging the vessel and complicating diplomatic efforts to bring the U.S. and Iran to Pakistan for talks.

video:video.video:title: In climate change fight, doomerism is out. Laughter is in
video:video.video:description: Across the world, groups of activists, teachers and psychologists are tackling one of the world’s most daunting problems — human-caused climate change — with laughter, dancing, hugs and most especially joy.

video:video.video:title: Warsh says he got no pressure from Trump to cut rates even as president publicly pushes for them
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump’s nominee to chair the Federal Reserve Kevin Warsh said Tuesday that he never promised the White House that he would cut interest rates, even as the president renewed his calls for the central bank to do so.

video:video.video:title: Climate doom and gloom? Try laughing instead. Activists embrace joy in the fight to save Earth
video:video.video:description: Across the world, groups of activists, teachers and psychologists are tackling one of the world’s most daunting problems — human-caused climate change — with laughter, dancing, hugs and most especially joy.

video:video.video:title: AP photographer reflects on the legacy of Chernobyl after 4 decades of coverage
video:video.video:description: Efrem Lukatsky, a photographer for The Associated Press, has chronicled the 1986 Chernobyl disaster from its earliest days, making many trips to the “exclusion zone” around the damaged nuclear power plant.

video:video.video:title: Khartoum remains quiet three years into the war in Sudan
video:video.video:description: Three years into the war and a year after Khartoum was taken over by the Sudanese army, the battered heart of the capital city remains quiet.

video:video.video:title: Pakistan maintaining optimism US-Iran talks can go ahead after ceasefire extension
video:video.video:description: Pakistan had planned to host a second round of talks, but the White House suspended Vice President JD Vance’s planned trip to Islamabad as Iran rebuffed efforts to restart negotiations.

video:video.video:title: Here's how to save money on flights as airlines raise prices amid the Iran war
video:video.video:description: For budget-conscious travelers, it’s gotten harder to escape the rising cost of airfare due to the war in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Lipstick and pajamas are among over 6,000 products that could cost more due to war
video:video.video:description: Did you know the war in Iran could raise the prices of some of your favourite everyday items? Spiking oil prices have been the biggest impact on those outside the conflict zone so far

video:video.video:title: Heavy rain and snowmelt is pushing large chunks of ice into northeastern Michigan homes
video:video.video:description: Homes have flooded along Black Lake in Michigan's northeastern Lower Peninsula as spring rains and winter melt overflow stressed dam systems

video:video.video:title: Uncertainty prevails in Tehran after Trump extends ceasefire indefinitely
video:video.video:description: Uncertainty over the future of the standoff with the U.S. prevailed in Tehran on Wednesday, hours after a decision by the U.S president to extend a ceasefire while keeping a naval blockade in place.

video:video.video:title: Chinese artist marks World Earth Day with annual performance art presentation
video:video.video:description: Wearing dinosaur-shaped balloons Chinese artist Kong Ning marks Earth Day in Beijing on Wednesday with her annual art installation performance.

video:video.video:title: Smallest deer, biggest aww: Pudu born at San Diego Zoo Safari Park
video:video.video:description: A newborn pudu fawn -- one of the world’s tiniest deer species -- has arrived at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park.

video:video.video:title: A robot is beating human pros at table tennis. Its maker calls it a milestone for machines
video:video.video:description: A paddle-wielding robot is so adept at playing table tennis that it is posing a tough challenge to elite human players and sometimes defeating them, according to a new study that shows how advances in artificial intelligence are making robots more agile.

video:video.video:title: Heated Rivalry' author and director on its crazy success and series future
video:video.video:description: The "Heated Rivalry" author and director-screenwriter were sensations at BookCon in New York. (April 22)

video:video.video:title: Wildfire burns in South Florida as the state faces one of its worst fire seasons in decades
video:video.video:description: Firefighters in Florida are battling 131 wildfires that have burned 34 square miles. A long drought, low humidity, and strong winds are fueling the fires.

video:video.video:title: Heavy rain and snowmelt are hurtling large chunks of ice into Michigan homes
video:video.video:description: Large chunks of ice rammed into homes along Michigan’s Black Lake in the northeastern Lower Peninsula as constant spring rainfall and winter melt left many homes flooded and threatened to overflow stressed dam systems.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo given colorful welcome before leading Mass in Mongomo in eastern Equatorial Guinea
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV held Mass in the eastern Equatorial Guinea city of Mongomo on Wednesday as he continued his visit to the country and his African tour.

video:video.video:title: Closely divided Congress reacts to Virginia redistricting result that could help Democrats
video:video.video:description: Republicans and Democrats in a closely divided Congress are reacting to Virginia voters approving a mid-decade redistricting plan that could boost Democrats’ chances of winning four additional U.S.

video:video.video:title: Virginia voters approve redistricting plan that could boost Democrats’ seats in Congress
video:video.video:description: Virginia voters approved a mid-decade redistricting plan on Tuesday that could boost Democrats’ chances of winning four additional U.S.

video:video.video:title: Georgia wildfires destroy dozens of homes, force evacuations
video:video.video:description: Brianna Elliot, who lives in Brantley County, Georgia, fears her home and her pets are gone after a rapidly-spreading wildfire engulfed the areas surrounding her home.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo offers message of hope to inmates on Equatorial Guinea prison visit
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV told inmates at one of Equatorial Guinea’s notorious prisons that they are not alone, as he drew attention Wednesday to prison conditions, human rights abuses and injustices that campaigners have denounced for years here.

video:video.video:title: Wildfires burn in Florida
video:video.video:description: Wildfires burning across the southeastern U.S. were intensifying today. Some of the biggest blazes are along Georgia’s coast and around Jacksonville, Florida, a state that is facing one of its worst fire seasons in decades.

video:video.video:title: Large wildfire burns in Clinch County, Georgia, prompting evacuations
video:video.video:description: Wildfires burning across the southeastern U.S. forced more people to flee Wednesday after destroying nearly 50 homes in Georgia and causing school closings.

video:video.video:title: Brazil’s 80-year-old Lula hits the treadmill to ease voter concerns about age
video:video.video:description: Brazil’s President Lula da Silva is boosting his reelection bid at age 80 by showcasing his fitness. Critics say his workouts are more popular than he is.

video:video.video:title: Streep, Hathaway dazzle at London 'Prada' premiere
video:video.video:description: The main cast of "The Devil Wears Prada" showed off their style at the film's London premiere. (April 22)

video:video.video:title: Runner collapses near Boston Marathon finish line and two competitors help him finish the race
video:video.video:description: Ajay Haridasse was close to finishing the Boston Marathon when his legs gave out. But as he struggled to get up, two other runners came along, lifted him up and helped him across the finish line.

video:video.video:title: CEO of Boston Athletic Association says 'camaraderie in running' evident at marathon
video:video.video:description: When a Boston Marathon runner's legs gave out, two other runners helped him cross the finish line. Jack Fleming, CEO of the Boston Athletic Association, says that type of kindness and camaraderie is typical among marathon runners.

video:video.video:title: More than 1,100 dancers gather for Radio City Rockettes auditions in New York
video:video.video:description: More than 1,100 dancers showed off their eye-high kicks and pirouettes as they auditioned for a spot with the iconic Radio City Rockettes.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 22
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, April 22nd: Iran fires on 3 ships in the Strait of Hormuz; Pope Leo XIV visits Equatorial Guinea; Warner shareholders set to vote on Paramount's $81 billion takeover; Sony's AI robot is beating human pros at table tennis.

video:video.video:title: Sweden's annual secondhand fashion swaps help to cut environmental waste
video:video.video:description: Sweden is trying to tackle garment waste by encouraging people to go for secondhand fashion. Clothes swapping events are growing in popularity as people reduce the environmental impact of their wardrobes.

video:video.video:title: Barry Levinson looks back on 'Sleepers'
video:video.video:description: Director Barry Levinson says "it's shocking" that it’s been 30 years since he made “Sleepers,” the 1996 drama starring Brad Pitt, Robert De Niro, Dustin Hoffman and Billy Crudup.

video:video.video:title: Groundbreaking begins on new LA2028 Olympics cricket stadium
video:video.video:description: Construction has begun on a new 10,000-seat stadium in Pomona, California, which will serve as the venue for men’s and women’s cricket during the Olympics in 2028.

video:video.video:title: 'Beauty will save the world': 30th Moscow Art fair opens with wide range of work on display
video:video.video:description: The 30th annual Art Moscow fair opened on Wednesday in Russia's capital. After starting out as a strictly modern art event in 1996, today among the fair's exhibits, were a wide specter of both classical paintings and contemporary art objects.

video:video.video:title: Doctors are using AI to improve and personalize ovarian cancer treatment
video:video.video:description: A global team of doctors and researchers are using AI to better improve the treatment and survival rates of women with ovarian cancer.

video:video.video:title: Volunteers offer free meals during Georgia wildfire evacuations
video:video.video:description: While wildfires are burning through Southeastern Georgia, a community gathered to help evacuees. (AP Video by Emilie Megnien)

video:video.video:title: Two killed and one in serious condition after chemical leak at a West Virginia plant, officials say
video:video.video:description: Authorities say a chemical leak at a West Virginia plant has killed two people and sent about 30 others to the hospital, including one person in serious condition.

video:video.video:title: Charlize Theron celebrates daughters’ differences, inspires fearless living
video:video.video:description: Charlize Theron walked the red carpet for her new Netflix film "Apex" Wednesday in New York. The actor, who did her own rock climbing in the action thriller, talked about the differences between her two daughters.

video:video.video:title: Iranian State TV airs what it says is video of Iran’s Navy seizing vessels in the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Iranian State TV on Thursday aired what it said was video of Iran’s Navy seizing vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Citizenship change has more Americans 'waking up' Canadian
video:video.video:description: A change in the Canada's citizenship laws has prompted a rush of Americans to explore their ancestry and file paperwork seeking dual citizenship.

video:video.video:title: Views across southern Lebanon show widespread destruction from Israel's razing of villages
video:video.video:description: Footage on Wednesday showed widespread destruction in parts of southern Lebanon following Israel's invasion of the area to carry out military operations against Hezbollah.

video:video.video:title: Fashionistas rejoice: Devil Wears Prada returns to Milan's big screens
video:video.video:description: As fashionistas gear up for the return of the Devil Wears Prada to cinemas, the buzz has hit Milan. The Italian city is the birthplace of the luxury designer brand immortalized in the movie's title.

video:video.video:title: Shipping ramifications of Iran war compound suffering for the sick in war-torn Sudan
video:video.video:description: In a small clinic in a remote village in rural Sudan, the shelves of the pharmacy were starting to empty out, with the clinic unsure about when the next shipment of medicines would arrive.

video:video.video:title: Celeste Rivas Hernandez, girl who singer D4vd is charged with killing, died from penetrating wounds
video:video.video:description: Celeste Rivas Hernandez, the 14-year-old girl, singer D4vd is charged with killing, died from penetrating injuries, according to an autopsy report released Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Shipping ramifications of Iran war compound suffering for the sick in war-torn Sudan
video:video.video:description: In a small clinic in a remote village in rural Sudan, the shelves of the pharmacy were starting to empty out, with the clinic unsure about when the next shipment of medicines would arrive.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians resort to burning cooking oil, plastic, to prepare meals and stay warm
video:video.video:description: Many shelter in makeshift tents and damaged buildings, using homemade stoves fueled by whatever scraps of wood, plastic and other materials that can be scavenged.

video:video.video:title: Navy Secretary John Phelan is leaving in latest departure of a top defense leader
video:video.video:description: Navy Secretary John Phelan is leaving his job, the Pentagon abruptly announced Wednesday, the first head of a military service to depart during President Donald Trump’s second term but just the latest top defense leader to step down or be ousted.
